@@ -638,6 +638,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-q21","title":"Task: Improve mobile document navigation","description":"Make navigating within and between documents effortless on mobile. ## Purpose With 40k+ words in the transcript, users need to navigate easily. Getting lost = abandonment. ## Current State - Reading progress bar exists - Table of contents exists (verify mobile experience) - Back-to-top button exists - Prev/Next document links at bottom ## Improvements Needed ### Table of Contents - Accessible from header (not just inline) - Bottom sheet presentation on mobile - Shows current section indicator - Tap to jump to section ### Section Headers - Consider sticky section headers during scroll - Or at minimum, clear visual section boundaries - Current section always knowable ### Progress Indicator - Verify progress bar visible but not intrusive - Consider percentage or 'X of Y sections' text ### Quick Actions - Easy access to TOC without scrolling up - Floating action button or header integration ### Navigation Between Documents - Verify prev/next cards work well on mobile - Consider swipe gesture (separate task) ## Files to Modify - Document viewer components - Possibly layout.tsx for header changes - New components if needed (e.g., mobile TOC sheet) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] TOC accessible within 1 tap from anywhere in document - [ ] Current section always visible/knowable - [ ] Can jump to any section in < 3 taps - [ ] Prev/next navigation clear and usable - [ ] No UX dead-ends","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:28:40.226724317-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:15:53.093590556-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:15:53.093590556-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented as part of brenner_bot-qka: Added MobileTocFab (floating button) that opens TOC in BottomSheet, added StickySectionIndicator showing current section number/title while scrolling with tap-to-open-TOC. Progress bar at top already existed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-q21","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-qka","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:29:18.141740211-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-q2uc","title":"Fix gemini prompt-builder delta format to match adversarial_critique schema","description":"prompt-builder gemini output used section 'critiques' and payload fields not recognized by delta parser. Align to adversarial_critique schema (name/attack/evidence/current_status) to avoid invalid deltas.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-02T09:23:39.794979674-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-02T09:23:59.263875162-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-02T09:23:59.263875162-05:00","close_reason":"Updated gemini prompt delta template to use adversarial_critique schema and adjusted tests."} {"id":"brenner_bot-q4u9","title":"TASK: Define Data Migration Strategy for Schema Changes","description":" ## Evolving the Data Model Without Losing User Data As the Brenner Loop evolves, the data schema will change: - Adding new fields to HypothesisCard - Restructuring EvidenceEntry - New session metadata Users have existing sessions in LocalStorage. We can't break them. ### Migration Architecture \`\`\`typescript // Version marker in all data interface SessionData { _version: number; // Schema version // ... rest of session } // Migration registry const MIGRATIONS: Record = { 2: migrateV1ToV2, 3: migrateV2ToV3, // etc. }; const CURRENT_VERSION = 3; // Migration function type type Migration = (data: unknown) => unknown; \`\`\` ### Migration Flow \`\`\` Load session from storage │ ▼ Check _version field │ ├── If current → Use directly │ └── If older → Run migrations │ ▼ Run each migration in sequence: v1 → v2 → v3 → current │ ▼ Save migrated data back │ ▼ Continue with session \`\`\` ### Example Migrations \`\`\`typescript // V1 → V2: Add confidence history function migrateV1ToV2(data: SessionV1): SessionV2 { return { ...data, _version: 2, hypotheses: data.hypotheses.map(h => ({ ...h, confidenceHistory: [{ confidence: h.confidence, timestamp: data.createdAt }] })) }; } // V2 → V3: Add prediction lock timestamp function migrateV2ToV3(data: SessionV2): SessionV3 { return { ...data, _version: 3, hypotheses: data.hypotheses.map(h => ({ ...h, predictions: h.predictions.map(p => ({ ...p, lockedAt: p.locked ? data.updatedAt : null })) })) }; } \`\`\` ### Migration Safety 1. **Backup before migration**: \`\`\`typescript async function safeLoadSession(id: string): Promise { const raw = localStorage.getItem(\`session:\${id}\`); const data = JSON.parse(raw); if (data._version < CURRENT_VERSION) { // Backup original localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${id}:backup:\${data._version}\`, raw); // Migrate const migrated = runMigrations(data); localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${id}\`, JSON.stringify(migrated)); return migrated; } return data; } \`\`\` 2. **Rollback capability**: \`\`\`typescript function rollbackMigration(sessionId: string, toVersion: number): boolean { const backupKey = \`session:\${sessionId}:backup:\${toVersion}\`; const backup = localStorage.getItem(backupKey); if (backup) { localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${sessionId}\`, backup); return true; } return false; } \`\`\` 3. **Version compatibility check**: \`\`\`typescript function isVersionSupported(version: number): boolean { return version >= MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION && version <= CURRENT_VERSION; } \`\`\` ### Testing Migrations \`\`\`typescript describe('Migrations', () => { test('V1 → V2 adds confidence history', () => { const v1Data = loadFixture('session-v1.json'); const v2Data = migrateV1ToV2(v1Data); expect(v2Data._version).toBe(2); expect(v2Data.hypotheses[0].confidenceHistory).toBeDefined(); expect(v2Data.hypotheses[0].confidenceHistory.length).toBe(1); }); test('Full migration path V1 → current', () => { const v1Data = loadFixture('session-v1.json'); const currentData = runMigrations(v1Data); expect(currentData._version).toBe(CURRENT_VERSION); expect(validateSession(currentData)).toBe(true); }); }); \`\`\` ### File Locations \`\`\` apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/index.ts apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/v1-to-v2.ts apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/v2-to-v3.ts apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/__tests__/ \`\`\` ### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Version field in all persisted data - [ ] Migration functions for each version bump - [ ] Backup before migration - [ ] Rollback capability - [ ] Migration tests with fixtures - [ ] Handles corrupted data gracefully ### Depends On - 1v26.1 (Session Data Model) - 1v26.2 (LocalStorage Persistence) ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:26:27.571132583-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T00:33:28.794732876-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T00:33:28.794732876-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented session schema migration framework (v0->v1), backups/rollback, tests, and safe persistence","labels":["brenner-loop","data-model","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-q4u9","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1v26.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:27:02.498882631-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-q4u9","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1v26.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:27:05.352437229-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-q5nn","title":"Vitest SSR Zod import failures in schema test files","description":"## Issue When running individual schema tests (assumption.test.ts, hypothesis.test.ts, prediction.test.ts, etc.), Vitest throws: ``` TypeError: undefined is not an object (evaluating '__vite_ssr_import_0__.z.enum') ``` ## Symptoms - 29 test files fail with Zod import errors - 141 test files pass - The import `import { z } from 'zod'` is correct in all files - Zod is installed (v3.25.76) - Other tests that use Zod pass (e.g., operators.test.ts) ## Affected Files - src/lib/schemas/assumption.test.ts - src/lib/schemas/assumption-lifecycle.test.ts - src/lib/schemas/hypothesis.test.ts - src/lib/schemas/prediction.test.ts - src/lib/schemas/scorecard.test.ts - src/lib/schemas/research-program.test.ts - And ~23 other schema test files ## Attempted Fixes - Cleared .vitest-cache (no effect) - Ran tests individually (still fail) ## Next Steps 1. Try `bun install` to refresh dependencies 2. Check for circular imports in schema files 3. Investigate Vite SSR module resolution 4. Compare working vs failing test file imports","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-06T15:14:32.532761341-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T15:14:47.353431737-05:00"} {"id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","title":"Task: Add Scorecard CLI Commands","description":"# Task: Add Scorecard CLI Commands ## Objective CLI commands for scoring sessions, viewing feedback, and comparing scores. ## Implementation Details ### Commands #### `brenner score ` ```bash brenner score RS-20251230 # Output: Session Score: RS-20251230 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Overall: 72/100 (C) Dimension Breakdown: Paradox Grounding ████████░░ 15/20 (75%) Hypothesis Kill Rate ██████████ 20/20 (100%) ★ Test Discriminability ██████░░░░ 10/15 (67%) Assumption Tracking ████████░░ 12/15 (80%) Third Alternative ████░░░░░░ 4/10 (40%) Experimental Feasibility ██████░░░░ 6/10 (60%) Adversarial Pressure ████░░░░░░ 5/10 (50%) Top Signals: ✓ At least one hypothesis killed (H-RS20251230-001) ✓ Kill linked to test result (T-RS20251230-002) ✗ "Both could be wrong" not considered ✗ Weak adversarial critique Run `brenner feedback RS-20251230` for detailed suggestions. ``` #### `brenner score --all` ```bash brenner score --all # Output (for all sessions in current program): Session Scores ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Session | Score | Grade | Trend ------------------|-------|-------|------- RS-20251230 | 72 | C | - RS-20251231 | 78 | C | ↑ RS-20260101 | 85 | B | ↑ RS-20260102 | 81 | B | ↓ Average: 79 (C) Trend: Improving ↑ Best: RS-20260101 (85, B) Worst: RS-20251230 (72, C) ``` #### `brenner feedback ` ```bash brenner feedback RS-20251230 # Output: Feedback for RS-20251230 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Summary: Session is progressing but has 3 areas to improve. [WARNING] Third Alternative Discovery (4/10) Issue: "Both could be wrong" not explicitly considered Suggestion: Explicitly ask: what if ALL current hypotheses are wrong? What third alternative would explain the data differently? Sydney Brenner says: "Both could be wrong." (§103) [WARNING] Adversarial Pressure (5/10) Issue: Insufficient critique of hypotheses Suggestion: Actively attack your hypotheses. What assumptions are you making? What would a skeptic say? [INFO] Test Discriminability (10/15) Issue: Some tests lack mutually exclusive predictions Suggestion: For each test, ensure hypotheses make DIFFERENT predictions. If H1 and H2 predict the same outcome, the test cannot distinguish them. ``` #### `brenner leaderboard` ```bash brenner leaderboard [--dimension=] [--limit=10] # Output: Brenner Method Leaderboard ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Top Sessions by Total Score: 1. RS-20260101 85 (B) ████████░░ 2. RS-20260102 81 (B) ████████░░ 3. RS-20251231 78 (C) ███████░░░ 4. RS-20251230 72 (C) ███████░░░ Best by Dimension: Paradox Grounding: RS-20260101 (18/20) Hypothesis Kill: RS-20251230, RS-20260101 (20/20 tie) Test Discrimin.: RS-20260102 (14/15) ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Score single session with dimension breakdown - [ ] Score all sessions with aggregation - [ ] Feedback shows prioritized suggestions - [ ] Leaderboard ranks sessions - [ ] Visual progress bars for percentages - [ ] Brenner quotes in feedback - [ ] JSON output for scripting ## Dependencies - Task: Implement Scoring Logic for All Dimensions - Task: Create Aggregation and Grade Functions - Task: Generate Actionable Feedback ## Effort Estimate ~300 lines of code, ~3 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:08.932720705-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T03:18:36.900715293-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T03:18:36.900715293-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented brenner score, feedback, and leaderboard commands with full 7-dimension scoring integration","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:50:56.742295246-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-yh8l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:50:56.960606025-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ls1v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:50:56.991501661-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-hh4z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:50:57.022966056-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:41:59.247166603-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-qka","title":"Feature: Mobile Reading Experience","description":"Optimize the experience of reading long-form content on mobile devices. ## Rationale The primary use case for BrennerBot on mobile is READING — users reviewing transcripts, distillations, or quotes. The transcript alone is 40k+ words. This needs to feel effortless. ## User Scenarios 1. Reading through a distillation during a commute (20-45 min) 2. Skimming the transcript to find a remembered quote 3. Comparing sections across documents 4. Reading in bed with dark mode ## Key Optimizations ### Typography - Ensure text doesn't require zooming (min 16px effective) - Optimal line length on narrow screens (45-65 characters) - Proper paragraph spacing for visual breathing room - Consider hyphenation for justified text (optional) ### Navigation Within Documents - Sticky section headers that show current position - Easy jump to section (table of contents) - Progress indicator (already exists, verify it works well) - 'Jump to top' accessible without scrolling up ### Visual Comfort - Proper contrast ratios for outdoor/bright light reading - Dark mode optimized for OLED screens (already have this) - No harsh transitions when scrolling ### Interaction - Smooth scrolling (verify momentum scrolling on iOS) - Section anchors that work on mobile - Easy copy/share of specific quotes ## Deliverables - Typography refinements for mobile - Document navigation improvements - Reading-specific UI enhancements ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Text readable without zooming on 375px width - [ ] Can navigate to any section within 2 taps - [ ] Progress indicator visible during reading - [ ] Dark mode doesn't cause eye strain - [ ] Smooth scrolling on iOS and Android","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:26:32.540075774-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:13:23.972747175-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:13:23.972747175-05:00","close_reason":"Mobile reading experience complete: sticky section indicator, mobile typography optimizations (16px+ clamp), reading-width constraints (65ch), momentum scrolling for iOS, MobileTocFab with BottomSheet for quick navigation. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qka","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ldh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:23.760040476-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qka","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-3vw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:24.994109586-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-qtrk","title":"Add demo mode logic to sessions page","description":"# Task: Implement Demo Mode in Sessions List Page ## Context Parent feature: Demo Sessions Page (brenner_bot-0y2e) DEPENDS ON: Demo fixture data task (brenner_bot-tdvc) ## Current Behavior (src/app/sessions/page.tsx) ```typescript let labLockedReason: string | null = null; if (!labModeEnabled) { labLockedReason = 'Lab mode is disabled. Set BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 to enable orchestration.'; } // ... {labLockedReason ? ( ) : ...} ``` When lab mode is disabled, users see a locked state with no useful content. ## Target Behavior When lab mode is disabled: 1. Show DemoSessionsView instead of LockedState 2. Demo sessions link to demo detail pages (handled by brenner_bot-vn19) 3. Keep existing behavior when lab mode IS enabled ## Code Changes Required ### 1. Import demo components and data ```typescript import { DemoSessionsView } from '@/components/sessions/DemoSessionsView'; import { getDemoThreadSummaries } from '@/lib/fixtures/demo-sessions'; ``` ### 2. Detect demo mode ```typescript const labModeEnabled = isLabModeEnabled(); const isDemoMode = !labModeEnabled; ``` ### 3. Update conditional rendering ```typescript // Instead of: {labLockedReason ? ( ) : ...} // Use: {isDemoMode ? ( ) : labLockedReason ? ( ) : ...} ``` ### 4. Adjust header for demo mode When in demo mode, maybe adjust the header to indicate it: ```typescript

Sessions {isDemoMode && (Demo)}

{isDemoMode ? 'Example sessions showing the Brenner Loop research workflow' : 'Browse and monitor Brenner Loop research sessions' }

{/* Keep New Session button - links to /sessions/new which has its own demo handling */}
``` ### 5. Consider the 'Local Sessions' section The page has a 'Local Sessions' section at the bottom that uses ``. This should probably remain visible in demo mode as it's client-side storage. ## Full Integration Flow 1. User visits /sessions on public site (no lab mode) 2. `isDemoMode = true` 3. Render DemoSessionsView showing demo session cards 4. User clicks a demo session card 5. Navigates to /sessions/demo-xxx 6. Detail page detects demo ID, renders demo content (brenner_bot-vn19) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Lab mode enabled: original behavior unchanged (fetches from Agent Mail) - [ ] Lab mode disabled: shows DemoSessionsView with demo sessions - [ ] Header indicates demo mode appropriately - [ ] 'New Session' button still visible and functional - [ ] 'Local Sessions' section still visible - [ ] No console errors - [ ] Works on Vercel preview deployment ## Testing - Local dev: toggle BRENNER_LAB_MODE to verify both paths - `BRENNER_LAB_MODE=1 bun run dev` → real mode - `bun run dev` (no var) → demo mode - Vercel preview: verify demo mode works (no lab mode in Vercel env) ## Dependencies - Demo fixture data (brenner_bot-tdvc) - DemoSessionsView component (brenner_bot-afui)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:54:38.152954927-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T11:26:37.549289722-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T11:26:37.549289722-05:00","close_reason":"Integrated DemoSessionsView into sessions list page, shows demo mode when lab mode disabled","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qtrk","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-tdvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T10:55:06.801821203-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
